comment_text.sn17.30:1.6: Even for an arahant, the burden of popularity and wealth takes them away from their meditation.

root_text.sn17.30:1.2: “Yopi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu arahaṁ khīṇāsavo tassapāhaṁ lābhasakkārasiloko antarāyāya vadāmī”ti.
root_text.sn17.30:1.3: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn17.30:1.4: “kissa pana, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno lābhasakkārasiloko antarāyāyā”ti?
root_text.sn17.30:1.5: “Yā hissa sā, ānanda, akuppā cetovimutti nāhaṁ tassā lābhasakkārasilokaṁ antarāyāya vadāmi.
root_text.sn17.30:1.6: Ye ca khvassa, ānanda, appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārā adhigatā tesāhamassa lābhasakkārasilokaṁ antarāyāya vadāmi.
root_text.sn17.30:1.7: Evaṁ dāruṇo kho, ānanda, lābhasakkārasiloko kaṭuko pharuso antarāyiko anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya.
root_text.sn17.30:1.9: ‘uppannaṁ lābhasakkārasilokaṁ pajahissāma, na ca no uppanno lābhasakkārasiloko cittaṁ pariyādāya ṭhassatī’ti.

translation_text.sn17.30:1.2: “Mendicants, possessions, honor, and popularity are an obstacle even for a mendicant who is perfected, with defilements ended.”
translation_text.sn17.30:1.3: When he said this, Venerable Ānanda said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn17.30:1.4: “Sir, what do possessions, honor, and popularity obstruct for a mendicant with defilements ended?”
translation_text.sn17.30:1.5: “Ānanda, I don’t say that possessions, honor, and popularity obstruct the unshakable freedom of heart.
translation_text.sn17.30:1.6: But I do say that possessions, honor, and popularity obstruct the achievement of blissful meditations in this life for a meditator who is diligent, keen, and resolute.
translation_text.sn17.30:1.7: So grim are possessions, honor, and popularity—bitter and harsh, an obstacle to reaching the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.sn17.30:1.8: So you should train like this:
translation_text.sn17.30:1.9: ‘We will give up arisen possessions, honor, and popularity, and we won’t let them occupy our minds.’
